title: Dilations: center
description: Given a figure and its image under a dilation, determine the dilation's center point.

assessment_items.question_content:
Triangle $M'$ is the image of triangle $M$ under a dilation.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the center of the dilation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find the **center of the dilation**, we can consider the lines that pass through corresponding vertices of triangles $M$ and $M'$. The place where these lines meet is the center of dilation. [[☃ explanation 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

The lines connecting corresponding vertices intersect at point $B$. Notice we only need two lines to find the center. We've given all three rays here to show how they all intersect at $B$.
The center of dilation is point $B$.

options.alt: A first quadrant coordinate plane. The pre-image and image of Triangles labeled M and M prime. The pre-image triange M has vertices at six, two, at eight, three, and at seven, five. The image triange M prime has vertices at zero, zero, at four, two, and at two, six. There are points A B C and D plotted with point A at twelve, six, point B at twelve, four, point D at twelve, ten, and point C at four, ten.

assessment_items.question_content:
Triangle $N'$ is the image of triangle $N$ under a dilation.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the center of the dilation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find the **center of the dilation**, we can consider the lines that pass through corresponding vertices of triangles $N$ and $N'$. The place where these lines meet is the center of dilation. [[☃ explanation 2]]

[[☃ image 1]]

The lines connecting corresponding vertices intersect at point $D$. Notice we only need two lines to find the center. We've given all three rays here to show how they all intersect at $D$.
The center of dilation is point $D$.

options.alt: A first quadrant coordinate plane. The pre-image and image of Triangles labeled N and N prime. The pre-image triange N has vertices at three, five, at four, five, and at four, three. The image triangle N prime has vertices at four, ten, at six, ten, and at six, six. There are points A B C and D plotted with point A at zero, four, point B at seven, twelve, point D at two, zero, and point C at eight, nine.
